title: Overview
text: Overview Website Analytics presents GA4 data in a focused desktop dashboard. The main screen is organized around locations, pages/downloads, trend activity, realtime activity, and summary cards. Report rows are held in memory only. Settings are stored locally in SQLite. The saved Google refresh token is encrypted with Windows DPAPI.

title: Quick Start
text: Quick Start First run Create a Google desktop OAuth client. Enter the client ID and secret in Settings. Confirm the redirect URI matches your OAuth desktop client. Connect Google once, then save settings. Normal use Choose a property and date range. Leave Auto 60 sec enabled for a live wallboard-style dashboard. Use Update for an immediate refresh.

title: Dashboard Tour
text: Dashboard Tour The dashboard has a compact hero section, summary cards, a location grid, a top pages/downloads chart, a users trend graph, and a realtime panel.

title: Website and Date Filters
text: Website and Date Filters Website property controls which GA4 property is queried. Date range controls standard reports, charts, and summary cards. All websites combines enabled properties. Realtime values come from GA4 realtime endpoints and may not exactly match standard reports at the same instant.

title: Users Graph
text: Users Graph The graph follows the selected date range. The axis uses whole numbers. Hover over a bar to read the exact value.

title: Visitor Locations
text: Visitor Locations The grid is sorted alphabetically for easier lookup. United States rows use city and state where GA4 returns them. International rows use city and country/region where available. (not set) means GA4 did not return that dimension.

title: Pages, Downloads, and Actions
text: Pages, Downloads, and Actions Home page is displayed when GA4 reports the path /. Download events appear when the website emits file_download or download-style events. The chart follows the selected date range.

title: Realtime Activity
text: Realtime Activity Active now is the current realtime active user count. Current location appears when GA4 returns city/country or city/state. Last activity is reset when the selected property changes.

title: Managing Properties
text: Managing Properties Use the numeric GA4 property ID. Do not use the Measurement ID that begins with G-. Add more properties as you add more websites.

title: Settings and Google Sign-in
text: Settings and Google Sign-in The client secret is hidden by default in the UI. The refresh token is encrypted locally. The app silently reconnects on startup when the saved token is valid. Google login appears only when the token is missing, revoked, or cannot be refreshed.

title: Running the Dashboard All Day
text: Running the Dashboard All Day Keep the app open and Auto 60 sec enabled. Use the status bar for the latest update message. If authentication expires, open Settings and reconnect Google.

title: Diagnostics
text: Diagnostics Use Diagnostics when Update fails. Check authentication state and selected property. Look at the last GA4 request message for permission or API errors.

title: Troubleshooting
text: Troubleshooting Common issues 403 permission errors usually mean the signed-in account cannot read that GA4 property. Zero data can be normal for quiet sites or dates with no GA4 traffic. Realtime and standard GA4 reports can update on different schedules. What to check OAuth client ID and secret. GA4 property ID. Google Analytics Data API enabled in Google Cloud. Tester/verification status for the OAuth consent screen.

title: Privacy and Storage
text: Privacy and Storage Settings and property definitions are local. Refresh tokens are encrypted for the current Windows user. GA4 report rows are not saved. The repository should track the schema, not a real SQLite database.
